bio.intro: I'm a Flutter Developer and Computer Engineering graduate with hands-on experience building real-world mobile applications across academic projects, hackathons, freelancing, and technical instruction.
bio.journey: Throughout my journey, I worked on multiple end-to-end applications, from idea and system design to implementation, testing, and deployment. My background in software engineering fundamentals combined with exposure to AI and machine learning enables me to build mobile solutions that are both scalable and data-aware.
bio.values: I value consistency over perfection, continuous learning, and building software that solves real problems. I'm especially interested in mobile roles where clean architecture, product thinking, and long-term code quality matter.

education.description: Comprehensive computer engineering education with focus on software engineering, algorithms, and mobile development.

usAccidents.title: US Accidents Analysis
usAccidents.subtitle: Distributed Big Data pipeline analyzing 7.7M accident records
usAccidents.overview: An end-to-end Big Data pipeline predicting accident severity and identifying risk factors using distributed computing and cloud orchestration.
usAccidents.challenge: Processing and analyzing a massive dataset (7.7M records) efficiently while identifying meaningful patterns in noisy traffic data.
usAccidents.approach: Implemented a distributed pipeline on GCP using PySpark and Mage. Designed a custom MapReduce Linear Regression for coefficient calculation and used dbt for star schema modeling.
usAccidents.solution: Achieved 84% prediction accuracy using Random Forest after reducing data processing time from 6 hours (Pandas) to 23 minutes (PySpark), with interactive dashboards for EDA insights.
contributions: Designed and implemented MapReduce Linear Regression from scratch
Led EDA phase: 80+ hours of data cleaning and correlation analysis
Configured Mage orchestration on GCP for 10+ ETL pipelines
Selected and tuned the champion Random Forest model (84% accuracy)

vpredicto.title: vPredicto
vpredicto.subtitle: Open-source library for State-of-the-Art Video Frame Prediction
vpredicto.overview: A unified Python library implementing cutting-edge VFP models like PredRNN++ and MIM under a single, standardized API.
vpredicto.challenge: Implementing complex spatiotemporal memory flows and gradient highway units for deep video prediction networks with high fidelity.
vpredicto.approach: Architected a modular system with a unified BaseModel. Implemented PredRNN++ with CausalLSTM cells and Memory In Memory (MIM) for differential tracking.
vpredicto.solution: Delivered a high-performance library achieving SSIM: 0.8734 and PSNR: 28.45 dB on MovingMNIST, enabling easy benchmarking of VFP models.
contributions: Designed the core BaseModel architecture and standardized API
Implemented CausalLSTM and Gradient Highway Units from research papers
Built efficient data pipelines for MovingMNIST and KTH datasets
Achieved state-of-the-art results for PredRNN++ and MIM implementations

phishing.title: Phishing Detection
phishing.subtitle: Machine Learning system for website security
phishing.overview: An ML system classifying websites as legitimate or phishing based on 30+ URL and structural features with 96% accuracy.
phishing.challenge: Effectively distinguishing between sophisticated phishing attempts and real websites while optimizing for low false-positive rates.
phishing.approach: Extracted 30+ features including HTTPS validity and domain age. Used SMOTE for class balancing and GridSearchCV for hyperparameter tuning.
phishing.solution: Developed a Random Forest ensemble model achieving 96% accuracy, significantly outperforming baseline logistic regression models.
contributions: Engineered 30+ URL and structural security features
Implemented SMOTE to handle highly imbalanced phishing datasets
Optimized Random Forest hyperparameters via GridSearchCV
Performed comparative analysis of 12 different ML architectures

vectorDb.title: Vectorized Database
vectorDb.subtitle: Similarity search engine built from scratch
vectorDb.overview: A custom vector database implementation demonstrating centroid-based clustering and high-speed similarity retrieval.
vectorDb.challenge: Implementing efficient similarity search that scales without the overhead of massive pre-built database engines.
vectorDb.approach: Used K-Means clustering to partition vectors into centroids. Implemented coarse and fine quantization for accelerated retrieval.
vectorDb.solution: A functional similarity engine that partitions large vector spaces and retrieves nearest neighbors using custom serialization for persistence.
contributions: Implemented K-Means clustering for vector partitioning
Designed coarse-to-fine quantization retrieval logic
Built custom CSV serialization for cluster persistence
Developed similarity search scoring using NumPy

eplReservation.title: EPL Ticket Reservation
eplReservation.subtitle: Public-facing booking system and admin dashboard
eplReservation.overview: A comprehensive system for English Premier League ticket booking, featuring a Flutter web admin dashboard and a .NET core backend.
eplReservation.challenge: Managing concurrent seat reservations and visualizing large datasets of revenue and occupancy in real-time.
eplReservation.approach: Built the admin dashboard using Flutter Web with Provider for reactive state management. Integrated fl_chart for revenue visualization.
eplReservation.solution: Delivered a polished dashboard with optimistic locking for seat selection and JWT-based security with automatic token refresh.
contributions: Developed 10+ dashboard screens using Flutter Web
Implemented JWT interceptor with silent auto-refresh logic
Built custom paginated data tables for 1000+ match records
Integrated interactive revenue and occupancy charts using fl_chart

ecosort.title: EcoSort Smart Recycling
ecosort.subtitle: IoT-enabled gamified recycling application
ecosort.overview: A freelance project transforming recycling into a game by connecting users to smart bins via Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE).
ecosort.challenge: Reverse-engineering a proprietary BLE protocol and implementing a secure, anti-fraud QR scanning system for rewards.
ecosort.approach: Used flutter_blue_plus for BLE communication and implemented HMAC-SHA256 signatures for QR validation. Integrated Firebase Custom Claims for RBAC.
ecosort.solution: Enabled the client to deploy 50+ smart bins, tracking thousands of recycling actions with a robust fraud prevention system.
contributions: Reverse-engineered the Blync smart bin BLE protocol
Implemented secure QR validation with HMAC-SHA256 signatures
Built role-based access control using Firebase Custom Claims
Developed the points calculation algorithm with streak bonuses

tapcash.title: TapCash FinTech
tapcash.subtitle: Modern financial management and data visualization
tapcash.overview: A polished fintech app prototype demonstrating secure mobile payments, spending insights, and wallet management.
tapcash.challenge: Creating a premium UI with complex data visualizations that remains performant on varied mobile hardware.
tapcash.approach: Built a custom widget architecture for financial indicators and used specialized charting libraries for spending insights.
tapcash.solution: A high-fidelity prototype delivered in a 2-week hackathon, recognized for its exceptional UI/UX by industry judges.
contributions: Designed the primary financial dashboard and spending 'Insights'
Implemented custom bottom navigation and reusable card widgets
Built reactive UI components for balance tracking
Presented the solution at Orange Digital Center's Eid Hackathon

searchEngine.title: Search Engine Architecture
searchEngine.subtitle: Distributed web crawler and TF-IDF ranker
searchEngine.overview: A distributed search engine built from scratch, implementing crawling, indexing, and ranked retrieval modules.
searchEngine.challenge: Building a high-concurrency web crawler that respects robots.txt and survives unexpected crashes.
searchEngine.approach: Implemented a multi-threaded crawler using Java's ExecutorService with HashSet-based deduplication and BFS state persistence.
searchEngine.solution: Successfully crawled 50,000+ pages at an average rate of 138 pages per minute with full fault tolerance.
contributions: Solo-implemented the 2000+ line multi-threaded crawler module
Built a robots.txt compliance engine and URL canonicalizer
Designed BFS queue persistence to disk to survive crashes
Collaborated on TF-IDF indexing logic and keyword highlighting

librarySystem.title: Central Library Management
librarySystem.subtitle: Enterprise SQL database design and management
librarySystem.overview: A complex management system for a city library network with 20+ tables managing branches, vendors, and borrowing.
librarySystem.challenge: Designing a normalized relational schema that handles recursive employee hierarchies and complex borrowing transactions.
librarySystem.approach: Designed a 3NF schema with 20+ tables. Implemented T-SQL triggers for fine calculations and stored procedures for ACID transactions.
librarySystem.solution: A robust database backend ensuring data integrity across library branches with automated fine tracking and inventory management.
contributions: Designed the normalized ER diagram and physical schema (20+ tables)
Implemented recursive CTEs for managing employee supervisor hierarchies
Built stored procedures for transactional book borrowing
Created T-SQL triggers for automated fine calculation

robotExploration.title: Multi-Robot Exploration
robotExploration.subtitle: Collaborative mapping in unknown environments
robotExploration.overview: A swarm robotics simulation using ROS where multiple robots cooperatively map an environment in Gazebo.
robotExploration.challenge: Synchronizing partial maps from multiple sensors into a single, cohesive global occupancy grid.
robotExploration.approach: Configured ROS multirobot_map_merge and tuned move_base parameters for obstacle inflation and costmap layers.
robotExploration.solution: Achieved efficient unknown environment mapping with multiple robots with minimal path overlap and collision-free navigation.
contributions: Configured map merging nodes for multi-robot synchronization
Tuned inflation radius and costmap parameters for MoveBase
Automated the Linux workspace setup for collaborative development
Performed Gazebo simulation testing and parameter calibration

lineFollower.title: Embedded CV Line Follower
lineFollower.subtitle: Mobile computer vision for autonomous navigation
lineFollower.overview: A smart car using an Android phone's camera for computer vision to calculate steering error for autonomous line following.
lineFollower.challenge: Processing real-time video frames on mobile at high frame rates while accurately extracting steering angles.
lineFollower.approach: Implemented an OpenCV pipeline in C++ via JNI for performance. Used Hough Line Transform with a custom line-merging algorithm.
lineFollower.solution: Successfully navigated a complex course with real-time CV processing and PID control on an ESP32 via TCP sockets.
contributions: Built the OpenCV C++ pipeline with Canny edge and Hough transforms
Designed a custom algorithm to merge fragmented line segments
Implemented the JNI bridge for zero-copy frame processing
Integrated TCP communication between Android and ESP32

mipsProcessor.title: MIPS 5-Stage Processor
mipsProcessor.subtitle: Pipelined CPU architecture in Verilog
mipsProcessor.overview: A complete 5-stage pipelined MIPS CPU featuring hazard detection, data forwarding, and branch prediction.
mipsProcessor.challenge: Resolving data hazards and timing conflicts across 5 pipeline stages while maintaining clock speed.
mipsProcessor.approach: Designed the Decode stage instruction lookup and control logic. Integrated forwarding paths to eliminate stalls.
mipsProcessor.solution: Achieved a CPI of 1.2 at 50 MHz, verified against 25+ MIPS programs including sorting and matrix multiplication.
contributions: Designed the complete instruction Decode stage and Register File
Generated all 15 core control signals for the execution pipeline
Debugged 40+ hours of waveform timing in ModelSim
Implemented EX and MEM data forwarding paths

circuitsSolver.title: Circuits Solver Engine
circuitsSolver.subtitle: SPICE-inspired simulator using nodal analysis
circuitsSolver.overview: A high-performance C++ circuit simulator using Modified Nodal Analysis (MNA) and the Eigen library.
circuitsSolver.challenge: Efficiently solving large systems of linear equations for arbitrary linear circuits with various sources.
circuitsSolver.approach: Designed a Component hierarchy with virtual MNA stamp methods. Integrated Eigen for sparse and dense matrix solving.
circuitsSolver.solution: A simulator capable of solving a 500-node circuit in 18ms, supporting passive components and dependent sources.
contributions: Solo-implemented the entire 1500+ line C++ simulation engine
Architected the Component hierarchy and MNA stamping logic
Integrated the Eigen library for high-speed linear algebra
Built a custom netlist parser for circuit definitions

logicSimulator.title: Logic Gate Simulator
logicSimulator.subtitle: GUI-based simulator with Command Pattern undo/redo
logicSimulator.overview: An interactive digital logic simulator with drag-and-drop gates, topological sorting, and event-driven simulation.
logicSimulator.challenge: Managing complex application states and providing robust user interaction features like multi-level undo.
logicSimulator.approach: Implemented the Command Pattern for all user actions. Used topological sorting to ensure correct signal propagation signal order.
logicSimulator.solution: A stable GUI application supporting multi-input gates, propagation delay simulation, and binary file serialization.
contributions: Architected the Application Manager and state machine
Implemented the Command Pattern for undo/redo (12 command types)
Built the event-driven simulation engine for efficient updates
Designed custom binary file format for save/load operations
